title: Advanced Algorithm Engineering Capstone · Correctness Defense
summary: Build and defend a planning engine across graph, optimization, and uncertain-load requirements. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for an advanced algorithm system with proof and operations packet. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare exact, approximate, randomized, and baseline candidates under one evidence model.
Connect proof obligations to executable invariants, oracle comparisons, and counterexamples.
Enforce time, memory, recursion, and output budgets under adversarial scale.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""python-dsa-2 algorithm evidence portfolio."""

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Solve a bounded quiz instance with 6 inputs. State the invariant, trace each state change, count the chosen operation, and retain the smallest counterexample.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for defend correctness, cost, alternatives, limits, and operational behavior to a technical review board.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Algorithm Portfolio correctness-and-cost proof for this quiz task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Algorithm Portfolio assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Review Defense: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Solve a bounded quiz instance with 8 inputs. State the invariant, trace each state change, count the chosen operation, and retain the smallest counterexample.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend correctness, cost, alternatives, limits, and operational behavior to a technical review board.
